description_long: This is a traditional Yixing-style teapot crafted from reddish-brown clay, featuring a squat, faceted body with a short, stout spout and a rounded handle. The teapot is adorned with intricate sculpted dragon motifs on the lid, handle, and spout, adding a sense of artistry and cultural symbolism. Chinese calligraphy is inscribed on one side of the body, enhancing its decorative appeal. The lid fits snugly and is topped with a sculpted dragon, serving as a functional and ornamental knob. The surface has a matte, slightly textured finish typical of unglazed clay teapots. Its overall appearance suggests it is both a functional vessel and a collectible display piece.
description: A reddish-brown, faceted clay teapot with sculpted dragon motifs and Chinese calligraphy, featuring a matte, slightly textured finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the dragon-adorned spout, handle, and lid, with visible Chinese calligraphy on the body.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlights the rounded handle with dragon motif and the lid's sculpted dragon knob.
description_view_2: Top view displays the circular lid with dragon sculpture and the faceted body, with both spout and handle visible.
description_view_3: Back angled view focuses on the handle and the rear body, with the lid's dragon knob prominent.
attribute_spout_orientation: Short, slightly curved spout projecting forward from the main body at a slight upward angle, positioned opposite the handle.
attribute_handle_design: Rounded loop handle with sculpted dragon head detail, attached at the upper and lower body, moderately thick for a secure grip.
attribute_lid_presence: Present; rounded, slightly domed lid with a sculpted dragon knob, fits snugly into the teapot opening.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Sculpted dragon motifs on lid, handle, and spout; Chinese calligraphy inscribed on the body; all in the same clay color.
